mechanism: Exposure to stimuli containing goal-related content leads to automatic activation of content-congruent semantic knowledge structures, which modulates subsequent behavioral performance through cognitive accessibility. Applied iteratively across trials with parametric stimulus manipulation.

Growth parameters estimated by latent variable models with multilevel structure: individual intercepts/slopes modeled as outcomes of group-level (school) and student-level (family SES) predictors. Nonlinear quadratic trajectories fit to IRT-scaled reading measures across sparse measurement occasions.
distribution: outcome: achievement growth; assumed: multivariate normal

mechanism: Context-level and person-level variables (e.g., autobiographical salience, arousal, personality traits, trait nostalgia) enter a two-level mixed-effects regression with cross-level interactions. Nostalgia intensity regressed on random coefficients of context-level predictors, with person-level intercept and slopes.

Cognitive load (working memory maintenance and distractor interference) manipulated orthogonally; performance accuracy measured as function of current and prior trial demand. Sequence effects (trial N performance conditioned on trial N-1 state) analyzed via repeated-measures ANOVA with factorial design.

Social class (subjective rank or income) enters multiple regression models predicting prosocial behavior (resource allocation, helping, trust). Mediation tested via linear regression with indirect effect estimation (Sobel test). Moderation by compassion or egalitarian values tested via interaction terms.
distribution: outcome: prosocial behavior; assumed: none stated

mechanism: Temporal frame (past vs. future) and temporal distance (near vs. far) manipulated as factors. Moral judgment or fairness rating regressed on frame and distance. Mediation via negative emotion tested: frame → emotion → judgment. Within-participants replication controls for isolation effects.
